mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into positive corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive return frequency above 52 percent over 60 trading days in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns during industrial production uptrends show no statistical edge over random baseline with Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI inflation exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in greater than 60 percent of high-inflation trigger events across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 45 percent of occurrences over a five-year backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, which directly supports corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDP growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 across all rolling three-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals contracting manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Twenty-day forward returns during industrial production contractions exceed positive return frequency of 55 percent in out-of-sample tests.
